questions.question: What is the sympathetic nervous system often compared to in this lesson?
questions.explanation: The sympathetic nervous system is compared to a 'gas pedal' because it screams 'GO!' when your brain senses a threat, flooding your body with adrenaline and cortisol for fight or flight.

questions.question: What does the parasympathetic nervous system do?

options.text: It increases heart rate and prepares you for danger
It actively calms your body, conserves energy, and helps you recover
It triggers the fight or flight response

questions.explanation: The parasympathetic nervous system is the 'brake pedal' or 'rest and digest' system. It says 'crisis over, you can relax now' and actively calms your body, conserves energy, and helps you recover.

questions.question: Why does your stomach feel like it has 'butterflies' during anxiety?

options.text: Because blood flow is redirected away from digestion to your muscles for survival
Because your stomach produces extra acid

questions.explanation: Those butterflies are your digestive system taking a break so your body can focus on survival. Blood flow is redirected away from digestion to your muscles, which is part of the sympathetic nervous system response.

questions.question: What is the goal of managing anxiety according to this lesson?

options.text: To eliminate all stress from your life
To get better at applying the 'brake pedal' and returning to balance

questions.explanation: The goal is not to eliminate stress but to get better at applying the 'brake' (parasympathetic nervous system). When you intentionally add calming tools to your day, you bring your system back into balance.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of activating your 'brake pedal'?

options.text: Deep breathing, a walk in nature, or listening to calming music
Working through lunch to finish a deadline

questions.explanation: Deep breathing, walking, listening to music, herbal tea, and time in nature are all examples of calming tools that activate the parasympathetic nervous system (brake pedal) and help restore balance.

title: Your Nervous System: The Gas and The Brake - Quiz
